name: tloz_ooa
game: The Legend of Zelda - Oracle of Ages

options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: goal
options.description:
The goal to accomplish in order to complete the seed.
- Beat Veran: beat the usual final boss
- Beat Ganon: teleport to the Room of Rites after beating Onox or Veran, then beat Ganon (same as linked game)
- Retrieve Maku Seed - You will have to retrieve the maku seed from the maku tree in order cut straight into the credits scene (similar to a triforce hunt in ALTTPR)

options.name: logic_difficulty
options.description:
The difficulty of the logic used to generate the seed.
- Casual: expects you to know what you would know when playing the game for the first time
- Medium: expects you to know well the alternatives on how to do basic things, but won't expect any trick
- Hard: expects you to know difficult tricks such as bomb jumps
- Hell: expects you to use tricks and glitches that span over more than a few inputs

options.name: required_essences
options.description:
The amount of essences that need to be obtained in order to get the seed from the Maku Tree and be able
to fight the final boss (depends on Goal Settings)

options.name: required_slates
options.description: The amount of slate that need to be obtained in order to get to the boss of the eigth dungeons.

options.name: warp_to_start_location
options.description:
This option changes the spot you warp in when you press select or start, A, and B buttons to warp back to the forest of time.
Please note that depending on the option you select, logic will be affected.

options.name: animal_companion
options.description:
Determines which animal companion you can summon using the Flute, as well as the layout of the Natzu region.
- Ricky: the kangaroo with boxing skills
- Dimitri: the swimming dinosaur who can eat anything
- Moosh: the flying blue bear (in seasons, he has a passion for Spring Bananas)

options.name: default_seed
options.description:
Determines which of the 5 seed types will be the "default seed type", which is given:
- when obtaining Seed Satchel
- when obtaining Slingshot or Seed Shooter
- by Horon (or Lynna) Seed Tree

options.name: linked_heros_cave
options.description:
Adds linked hero's cave to a list of locations for you to complete. This option also allows you to mark which location the linked hero's cave will be in.
- Maku Tree Entrance Right Side: A cave will be placed to the right side of the maku tree entrance, allowing access despite the finished game flag not being set.

options.name: duplicate_seed_trees
options.description:
The game contains 8 seed trees, but only 5 types of seeds. This means that some types of seeds can appear on
multiple trees. This setting lets you choose seed trees that will be guaranteed to not hold a unique type of
seed. You can choose up to 3.
Regardless of what you choose, each seed type will appear on at most 2 trees.
Valid choices are:
- Lynna City
- Ambi's Palace
- Deku Forest
- Symmetry City
- Crescent Island
- Rolling Ridge West
- Rolling Ridge East
- Zora Village

options.name: shuffle_dungeons
options.description:
If enabled, each dungeon entrance will lead to a random dungeon picked at generation time.
Otherwise, all dungeon entrances lead to their dungeon as intended.

options.name: master_keys
options.description:
- Disabled: All dungeon keys must be obtained individually, just like in vanilla
- All Small Keys: Small Keys are replaced by a single Master Key for each dungeon which is capable of opening
  every small keydoor for that dungeon
- All Dungeon Keys: the Master Key for each dungeon is also capable of opening the boss keydoor,
  removing Boss Keys from the item pool
Master Keys placement is determined following the "Keysanity (Small Keys)" option.

options.name: lynna_gardener
options.description:
When enabled, a friendly gardener will have trimmed the bushes outside of Lynna City and cleared the path
so you don't have to! This will expand the sphere 0 checks to include everything past the bushes that you
normally would need nothing for.

options.name: keysanity_small_keys
options.description: If enabled, dungeon Small Keys can be found anywhere instead of being confined in their dungeon of origin.

options.name: keysanity_boss_keys
options.description: If enabled, dungeon Boss Keys can be found anywhere instead of being confined in their dungeon of origin.

options.name: keysanity_maps_compasses
options.description: If enabled, Dungeon Maps and Compasses can be found anywhere instead of being confined in their dungeon of origin.

options.name: keysanity_slates
options.description: If enabled, Slates can be found anywhere instead of being confined in Dungeon 8.

options.name: required_rings
options.description:
Forces a specified set of rings to appear somewhere in the seed.
Adding too many rings to this list can cause generation failures.
List of ring names can be found here: https://zeldawiki.wiki/wiki/Magic_Ring

options.name: excluded_rings
options.description:
Forces a specified set of rings to not appear in the seed.
List of ring names can be found here: https://zeldawiki.wiki/wiki/Magic_Ring

options.name: shop_prices_factor
options.description:
A factor (expressed as percentage) that will be applied to all prices inside all shops in the game.
- Setting it at 10% will make all items almost free
- Setting it at 500% will make all items horrendously expensive, use at your own risk!

options.name: advance_shop
options.description:
In the vanilla game, there is a house (northwest of Horon Village in Seasons, and south of the maku tree entrance in Lynna Village for Ages) hosting the secret "Advance Shop" that can only
be accessed if the game is being played on a Game Boy Advance console.
If enabled, this option makes this shop always open, adding 3 shop locations to the game (and some rupees to the
item pool to compensate for the extra purchases that might be required)

options.name: combat_difficulty
options.description:
Modifies the damage taken during combat to make this aspect of the game easier or harder depending on the
type of experience you want to have

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
